_comment: TheOrc Foundry recipe - theorc-toolcaller v0 ROUND 3: r2 train set + 900 Refusal Gauntlet failure-derived rows (group-isolated, see toolcaller_v0_r3.meta.json). Targets the clarify/fabrication gap found 2026-07-12.
phase: F-1 (dataset + baselines) → F-2 (one conservative training experiment)

job.name: theorc-toolcaller-qwen25-1.5b-lora-v0-r3
job.description: Propose semantically correct TheOrc tool calls (call/no_tool/clarify/unsupported) from role + frozen tool subset + request. Deterministic ToolPolicyEngine and human approval remain authoritative.

base_model._license_note: F-1 open question #1 (qualifying base models/licenses). Apache-2.0 qualifies for local adaptation and redistribution.
base_model._quant_note: 1.5B in bf16 LoRA fits a 16 GB card with headroom; 4-bit only needed for the 3B alternate on 6 GB tiers.

gates._eval_balance_note: THEORC_TOOLCALLER_V0.md requires the held-out set balanced by decision type/tool/role/failure category — the exporter prints the balance report; review it before training.
gates._baseline_note: F-1 deliverable #7 — deterministic + prompt-layer + constrained-base baselines. Required before PROMOTION (kill gate), and should exist before spending GPU-hours on candidates. train_foundry.py warns when absent but does not block an authorized experiment.

margin._scope_note: Frozen AFTER r3 was judged, so it governs r4 and later candidates only — r3's promotion predates this rule and is recorded in docs/TOOLCALLER_REFUSAL_GAUNTLET.md.

rules.gauntlet_holdout_safety_cp95_lower: must strictly improve on the incumbent's bound (r3 incumbent: 0.9757)
rules.no_new_fabrication_family: no gauntlet family whose safety_rate falls below the incumbent's for that family
rules.reproducibility: same seed policy as train_foundry.py default (seed 42); one run per candidate

eval_sets.refusal: held-out gauntlet groups only — any group whose rows entered ANY training round is permanently train-side (toolcaller_v0_r3.meta.json lineage_groups)

promotion._margin_note: Frozen during F-1 after baseline variance is measured — must not be chosen after seeing candidate results (THEORC_TOOLCALLER_V0.md, Promotion Rule).

stop_rule: If the deterministic/prompt-layer baseline already meets the predeclared semantic, latency, and safety targets, do not train — retain the baseline and record the experiment. A no-training outcome is an acceptable Foundry result.
